Paul Michael Levesque, better known by his ring name Triple H, started his career in World Championship Wrestling (WCW) in 1994, where he was given the name Terra Ryzing, a play on the word “terrorizing.”He later changed his name to Hunter Hearst Helmsley, a snobbish and arrogant character, before joining the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) in 1995.He shortened his name to Triple H in 1998 and became one of the most influential and successful wrestlers in the company’s history.HHH has won various titles during his tenure, such as the Intercontinental Championship five times, the World Tag Team Championship twice, the Unified WWE Tag Team Championship once, the European Championship twice, and the world championship fourteen times, making him the seventh Triple Crown Champion and the second Grand Slam Champion in WWE history.He is also a two-time winner of the Royal Rumble match and the 1997 King of the Ring tournament.In addition to his wrestling career, Triple H has gained prominence for his behind-the-scenes role in WWE, as he is the Executive Vice President of Global Talent Strategy & Development, the founder and senior producer of NXT, and the creator of the WWE Performance Center. Hulk Hogan debuted in the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) in 1979 when he was signed by the owner Vince McMahon Sr., and his first victory was against the legendary André the Giant.He rose to fame in 1984, when he won the WWF Championship from the Iron Sheik and became the face of the company.Hogan rapidly gained a lot of stardom, along with legendary terms like Hulkamania.He has won twelve world championships in his career, including six WWF/WWE Championships and six WCW World Heavyweight Championships.Hogan was the first wrestler to win consecutive Royal Rumble matches in 1990 and 1991.He is also the second Triple Crown Champion and the fifth Grand Slam Champion in WWE history.He became a household name in the United States in the mid-1980s, and his image was used to endorse various products and merchandise. He also ventured into acting and starred in several movies and television shows.He played the lead role in the movie ‘No Holds Barred in 1989, which was co-produced by WWF. He also appeared in other films such as ‘Mr. Nanny’ in 1993 and ‘Santa with Muscles’ in 1996.Hulk Hogan is widely regarded as one of the most influential and no doubt one of the top 10 WWE wrestlers of all time and a true legend in his own right. Ric Flair made his debut in 1972 in Rice Lake, Wisconsin, where he faced George Gadaski in a match that ended in a draw. He soon adopted the nickname “Nature Boy,” a persona that embodied his flamboyant and charismatic style.He had a turbulent life that included a strained relationship with his adoptive parents, various injuries, four failed marriages, and the tragic death of his son.In a career spanning over 40 years, he has broken several records and achieved numerous championships.Ric Flair has been recognized as a 16-time world champion, including eight NWA World Heavyweight Championships, six WCW World Heavyweight Championships, and two WWF Championships.Flair is the first two-time WWE Hall of Famer, having been inducted individually in 2008 and as a member of The Four Horsemen in 2012.He is also the first Triple Crown Champion and the first Grand Slam Champion in WWE history.Flair’s legacy has influenced many generations of wrestlers, and he has been praised for his contributions to the sport.He continued to wrestle for Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) even after officially retiring from WWE in 2008, but he later admitted that wrestling for TNA was the “number one regret of his career.”He is currently seen in WWE to support his daughter, Charlotte Flair, who is also a successful wrestler and a 13-time women’s champion. John Cena is an American actor, television presenter, and perhaps the greatest WWE wrestler.His wrestling career began in 2000 when he won the Ultimate Pro Wrestling (UPW) Heavyweight Championship in California and earned the nickname “The Prototype” for his physique and style.He signed a contract with the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) in 2001 and was sent to Ohio Valley Wrestling (OVW) for training.John Cena won the OVW Heavyweight Championship in 2002 and made his debut on the main roster in June 2002, where he joined the popular show SmackDown.Cena rose to prominence in 2004, when he defeated Big Show for the United States Championship at WrestleMania XX and established himself as a fan favorite.He has won various titles and accolades during his tenure, such as the WWE Championship a record 13 times, the World Heavyweight Championship three times, the WWE Tag Team Championship four times, and the Money in the Bank ladder match in 2012.He is the fifth Triple Crown Champion and the third Grand Slam Champion in WWE history.He is also a two-time winner of the Royal Rumble match in 2008 and 2013, and the first wrestler to defeat Umaga in 2007. Shawn Michaels debuted in 1988 in the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) after starting his wrestling career in the Universal Wrestling Federation (UWF) and American Wrestling Association (AWA).He won the AWA World Tag Team Championship twice with his partner Marty Jannetty, who formed a tag team called The Rockers.He later became a singles performer and adopted a cocky and charismatic persona that made him a fan favorite.He had a significant impact on booking decisions as the leader of the backstage group called The Kliq, which consisted of other influential wrestlers such as Scott Hall, Kevin Nash, Sean Waltman, and Triple H.The group was involved in a controversial incident in 1996, known as The Curtain Call, which exposed the scripted nature of wrestling.In the same year, Michaels teamed up with Triple H, Rick Rude, and Chyna to form a faction called D-Generation X (DX), which was famous for their rebellious and crude humor. Stone Cold Steve Austin rose to fame in the late 1990s and early 2000s, when he became the most popular star in the World Wrestling Federation (WWF), now known as WWE.He adopted a rebellious and anti-authority persona, which led him to feud with the WWE Chairman Vince McMahon, in one of the greatest controversies in history.Stone Cold has stowed many achievements in the wrestling industry, thus placing him as one of the top WWE wrestlers of all time.He achieved 19 championships during his vocation, where Stone was given the honorable title of the popular World Heavyweight Championship six times, a double-cross Intercontinental Title, along a Tag Team Champion four times in a row.Stone Cold was also awarded the Triple Crown champion in World Wrestling Entertainment.He even bagged the King of the Ring competition in 1996, just like the 1998, 1997, and 2001 Royal Rumble championships, which made him the chief personality to win the battle thrice. Dwayne Johnson started his wrestling career in 1996 in the World Wrestling Federation (WWF), now known as WWE, where he was given the name Flex Kavana. He fought against famous wrestlers like The Brooklyn Brawler and Bart Sawyer and won several matches. He later changed his name to The Rock and became one of the most popular and charismatic wrestlers in the company. He had a notorious feud with Stone Cold Steve Austin, who was his main rival and ally. He won 17 different WWE titles during his tenure.He starred in several blockbuster movies like A Supernatural Bad Guy, Scorpion King, Game Plan, etc.Johnson’s acting vocation kept on flourishing through his appearances in several movies, such as Driver (2010) and the most popular Fast and Furious 6 in 2013.In 2014, movie directors featured him as a tough man in Hercules.He additionally came back to wrestling brawls every once in a while, incorporating a 2014 collaboration with the famous Stone Cold and Hulk Hogan at WrestleMania 30. Many WWE fans, critics, and other athletes regard The Undertaker best WWE wrestler of all time.In 1989, he bagged the title of ‘Master of Pain’ in the USWA Unified World Heavyweight Championship.He achieved several WWF titles in 1991, 1997, 1999, and 2002, respectively.He was also the winner of the WWF Hardcore Championship in the year 2001.The Undertaker’s presentation in the 2007 ‘Royal Rumble’ battle is considered one of his most energetic ones. This event featured five expert wrestling matches, and The Undertaker won the title by defeating Shawn Michaels.He also achieved many other feats that no one else has matched. Over the years, The Undertaker has earned various less celebrated honors that none of his peers can claim.He has defeated many top stars on the biggest stages and has beaten every member of one of the company’s most prestigious groups. The 10 most successful wrestlers in WWE history listed in this article represent the pinnacle of in-ring prowess, charisma, and overall impact on the industry.From the flamboyant showmanship of Hulk Hogan to the high-flying acrobatic feats of Randy Orton, professional wrestling boasts a rich history of larger-than-life characters who have captivated audiences for generations.While countless athletes have stepped into the squared circle over the years, only a select few have truly transcended the sport, etching their names forever in the annals of wrestling history. Roman Reigns is currently the biggest and most famous WWE Superstar. His real name is Leati Joseph Joe Anoa’i, but he is better known worldwide as Roman Reigns. Born in 1985, Roman Reigns debuted in 2010. However, he did not achieve much success in his early wrestling career. After 2012, he became very popular after joining The Shield alongside Dean Ambrose and Seth Rollins. Roman Reigns is a very famous and beloved WWE Superstar in India. He has achieved great success in his life in a very short time. Reigns has won the World Heavyweight Championship three times, the United States Championship once, and the Tag Team Championship once. Additionally, Roman Reigns was the Superstar of the Year in 2014. Today, Roman Reigns is not only a successful wrestler but also a successful Hollywood actor. Paul Michael Levesque is the real name of Triple H. In 1994, Triple H debuted in professional wrestling. Triple H became famous in a short time due to his attractive appearance. Triple H is considered one of the greatest wrestlers of all time. He weighs about 110 kg and is 6 feet 4 inches tall. Triple H had his debut match in 1992. Triple H has won 25 championships, including 9 WWE Championships and 5 World Heavyweight Championships. Stephanie McMahon married Triple H in 2003; she is the daughter of WWE Chairman and CEO Vince McMahon. Triple H is undoubtedly one of the most famous and well-known WWE wrestlers of all time, with a large fan base in India. Anyone who enjoys watching WWE will be familiar with the name The Great Khali. His real name is Dalip Singh Rana, and he is Indian. The Great Khali is a resident of Dhirina, a town in the Sirmaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India. Today, The Great Khali is a famous name in WWE, known by almost everyone in India. Khali began his professional wrestling career in 2000. However, he became truly famous when he started his WWE career in 2006. Thanks to his very tall stature and large physique, Khali quickly became popular in WWE. In WWE history, Khali is one of the most beloved wrestlers. In 2007, in a triple threat match, Khali defeated Kane and Batista, two of WWE’s legends, to win the WWE World Heavyweight Title. Khali became the first Indian to become a World Heavyweight Champion. Khali is 7 feet 4 inches tall and weighs about 190 kg. Besides wrestling, Khali has appeared in 4 Hollywood movies, 2 Bollywood movies, and 3 television shows. In conclusion, the WWE has seen numerous extraordinary wrestlers over the years, each leaving a unique mark on the sport. Among the WWE best player, names like The Undertaker, John Cena, The Rock, Hulk Hogan, Ric Flair, Stone Cold Steve Austin, Shawn Michaels, Roman Reigns, Triple H, and The Great Khali stand out for their incredible achievements and immense popularity. These superstars have not only defined eras in wrestling but have also crossed over into mainstream entertainment, becoming global icons. Whether through their championship victories, unforgettable matches, or larger-than-life personas, these wrestlers have solidified their places as the best players in WWE history.

title: 35 Greatest WWE Superstars in History [Ranked]
meta_description: The 35 Greatest WWE Superstars in History Have Been Named & Ranked - in Order
headings: Subscribe to the newsletter for deeper WWE all-time rankings
content: Nick is a combat sports writer and editor for GIVEMESPORT - with eight years of experience in the industry. Nick started out with the GIVEMESPORT Academy in 2018 and has also previously worked as a combat sports editor for Sportskeeda. In his current role with the site, Nick produces and edits news pieces, features and evergreen articles on a wide variety of sports. A fan of the sweet science since childhood, Nick has attended over 100 boxing and UFC events including Manny Pacquiao vs Ricky Hatton, Floyd Mayweather vs Miguel Cotto, Deontay Wilder vs Tyson Fury II, Anthony Joshua vs Oleksandr Usyk and Canelo vs Crawford. When not writing about boxing or MMA, Nick can be found watching cricket, rugby and darts, as well as cheering on Leeds United at Elland Road. Throughout its illustrious history, WWE has seen some legendary professional wrestlers step into its rings worldwide. The likes of Hulk Hogan, Shawn Michaels, and The Undertaker captured the hearts of fans during some of the company's most popular periods, while the likes of Roman Reigns came onto the scene much more recently and have done a brilliant job flying the WWE flag. With such an army of impressive and iconic figures having called themselves WWE Superstars over the years, compiling a list of the 35 greatest sounds like an incredibly tough task and one no one would likely rush to create. With that being said, that's exactly what we've decided to do. Looking at several different components, we've identified the 35 best wrestlers in WWE history. Before we get started, take a look at the different components we've looked at to create our list below. Now that's out of the way, let's get started. Here are the 35 greatest WWE wrestlers of all time, ranked. Starting the list is Batista. The Animal did a lot within the squared circle during his time as a WWE Superstar, mastering the art of being both a heel and a face. A product of Ohio Valley Wrestling (OVW), coming up at the same time as Brock Lesnar and John Cena, his career took off in 2003 when he was placed in the iconic Evolution faction. With a short but sweet CV of achievements, Batista skipped the mid-card titles and only focused on World Championship success. A six-time World Champion, he also found the time for four reigns as a Tag Team Champion. Having been teased with a Hall of Fame induction for a few years, it is more than deserving for what he produced during his career. A current WWE Superstar, Sheamus has only started to receive his flowers in recent years. One of the toughest in-ring competitors WWE has ever had, the Irishman has been a mainstay on the product for nearly two decades. Arriving in the States in 2006, the Celtic Warrior has had numerous ups and downs but has always managed to weather the storms. A four-time World Champion, King of the Ring, Money in the Bank, and Royal Rumble winner, his makeshift tag team with Cesaro provided him with five reigns as a Tag Team Champion. Boasting a list of matches that rank as some of WWE's best, he is only one title away from coveted Grand Slam status. The Charismatic Enigma is next on our list, and despite Jeff Hardy enjoying a fantastic career within the squared circle, he still remains one of WWE's biggest 'What ifs?'. Reaching immense levels of popularity around 2008, troubles outside the ring caught up with Jeff and his career at a main event level ended up paying for it. Regardless, the tag team specialist will still go down in history. One-half of one of WWE's most successful tag teams, The Hardy Boyz, the current TNA wrestler, also forged a thriving singles career for himself. A three-time World Champion and five-time Intercontinental Champion, alongside a plethora of other achievements, Jeff Hardy will forever be adored by the WWE Universe. Razor Ramon, also known as Scott Hall, is a two-time Hall of Fame entrant remembered for his 'bad guy' persona. A charismatic wrestler, Ramon's patented slicked-back hair and toothpick made sure no one changed the channel when he was on screens. A four-time Intercontinental Champion, he is regarded as one of the best wrestlers to hold the belt, giving it the prestige it still holds to this day. One-half of one of WrestleMania's best matches, a Ladder Match with Shawn Michaels at WrestleMania X, Ramon's blend of athleticism, charm, and crowd work makes him one of the best wrestlers in WWE history. Entertaining fans, regardless of whether he was a face or heel, he made it cool to be hated. One of the most deserving wrestlers of a World Championship run that never happened, he is living proof that hard work pays off and dreams do come true. One of the greatest women's wrestlers of all time, Trish Stratus is a trailblazer who paved the way for women's success in WWE. The Canadian-born wrestler holds the record for the most WWE Women's Championships reigns, with seven, and is one of only four women's wrestlers to have a reign last over a year. Managing to transcend away from countless suggesti

Entertaining fans, regardless of whether he was a face or heel, he made it cool to be hated. One of the most deserving wrestlers of a World Championship run that never happened, he is living proof that hard work pays off and dreams do come true. One of the greatest women's wrestlers of all time, Trish Stratus is a trailblazer who paved the way for women's success in WWE. The Canadian-born wrestler holds the record for the most WWE Women's Championships reigns, with seven, and is one of only four women's wrestlers to have a reign last over a year. Managing to transcend away from countless suggestive storylines, her feud with Lita put women's wrestling on the map as something that can be treated as equal to men's. Stratus retired in 2006, regarded as one of the best women's wrestlers ever, and it was an opinion that was validated upon her 2022 return. Making full-time appearances at 46 years old, she won the Tag Team Championships and looked as if she had somehow gotten better in the ring. A 2013 Hall of Fame entrant, Stratus, and many women's wrestlers of that era, will always be held in high regard for what they did for the industry. The Ninth Wonder of the World, Chyna's nickname perfectly describes her. A towering wrestler, she was one of the first women's wrestlers to possess a size that presented her as equal, if not more intimidating, than her male counterparts. It is because of this that she became the first and only women's wrestler to hold the Intercontinental Championship. Remembered for her work alongside D-Generation X, she paved the way for the likes of Rhea Ripley, wrestlers who shouldn't feel shame but be celebrated for being larger than life. A 2019 entrant into the Hall of Fame, as well as a former Women's Champion, Chyna was voted as the fourth-greatest WWE Women's Superstar in 2021. Remembered for beating the best of the men's and women's divisions, history has been kind to Chyna. CM Punk's WWE career can be split into two separate runs. Both of those being iconic, he achieved unimaginable success during his first stint as a WWE Superstar. Moving to the Stamford-based giant after success on the independent circuit, many doubted Punk had the right look or attitude to succeed. However, five reigns as World Champion later, and some of WWE's greatest matches, and the Best in the World proved why his nickname is so. Returning to the company in 2023 after nearly a decade away, the Voice of the Voiceless hasn't lost a step. Embarking on a fantastic feud with Drew McIntyre, Punk achieved his lifetime goal of main-eventing WrestleMania in 2025. One of WWE's most popular women's wrestlers, Becky Lynch, has reached unforeseen heights throughout her career as a WWE Superstar. Starting off at NXT in 2013, she was always viewed as the fourth member of the Four Horsewoman, with the other members garnering more attention. However, following the women's revolution and a successful call-up to the main roster, The Lasskicker became one of WWE's most transcendent stars in modern history. Enjoying a run as The Man and Big Time Becks, she is a seven-time Women's World Champion on the main roster, with a litany of other achievements padding a Hall of Fame-worthy career. Still an active wrestler, Seth Rollins has achieved everything a WWE Superstar could dream of. Emerging as the next big thing following his work as a part of The Shield, his iconic WrestleMania 31 cash-in stands the test of time. With numerous title reigns under his belt, Rollins continues to wrestle as a main event act, evolving as the years have passed. An immense wrestler and just as equally good on the mic, he is one of WWE's most reliable acts. Having spent the latter part of his career being the bridesmaid and not the bride, Rollins was viewed as the perfect act to put people over or to raise people to the main event scene. With a Hall of Fame entry awaiting him in the future, The Visionary is one of the most memorable acts within modern WWE programming. Charlotte Flair is arguably the greatest women's wrestler in WWE history. Daughter to Ric Flair, Charlotte has carved out an unmatchable level of success within the women's division. Holding the most combined title reigns of any women's wrestler, she is also the first and only two-time women's Royal Rumble winner. From her matches with her fellow Four Horsewomen stablemates, to a WrestleMania great with Rhea Ripley, she has an all-timer CV of matches to her name. Still an active competitor, Charlotte's immense success sees her regarded as her own act, something second-generation wrestlers struggle to do. With an in-ring ability that continues to amaze the WWE Universe, Charlotte somehow makes it look easy. Daughter to the legendary Ric Flair, Charlotte's career is yet to be over and is already one of the greatest.

title: WWE Superstars: Top-10 Best WWE Wrestlers 2020 | SportyTell
headings: The Best WWE Wrestlers Right Now
content: World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) is home to some of the best professional wrestlers in the world. They have thrilled their fans with symbolic moves, incredible showmanship, and surprisingly outstanding performances. The franchise prides itself on having some of the biggest superstars in the business among its ranks. Still, the subject of the best wrestlers of the year has been the subject of controversy, with every WWE fan holding their own opinion. However, choosing the best WWE Wrestlers for 2020 is quite complicated due to the coronavirus outbreak. Since we have to decide to rank the 10 best WWE Wrestlers of 2020, the following criteria are what we consider very crucial in ranking these heels: Presently, when it comes to style and shocking victories, you can always count on Bianca Belair. She is one of the most supported female wrestlers globally, and it’s no surprise to see her on our list. After her victory and outstanding performance against Shayna Baszler, she became the talk of the town among wrestling fans. In 2018, she was ranked at number nine in the top 10 female wrestlers of the year due to her outstanding performance. Before forming DIY with Johny Gargano, Ciampa was highly praised as the biggest heel-turn in NXT history, and he since demonstrated all his charisma in building a fantastic character. He was formerly known as Psycho Killer due to the different ways he mentally disturbed his opponents. Later, Ciampa proved to be as good a heel as he combines his talent inside the ring to become one of the best fighters in the world. There are few who compare to him in terms of consolidation between character and skill within the rings. Since her return from her injury in 2012, she has latched her way to the top with astonishing victories and styles. She has won the SmackDown Women’s Champion title a total of three times in her career. Becky Lynch made her appearance on the cover page of ESPN magazine-making her the first wrestler to be featured. Lynch is not just a professional fighter but a born champion as she is the only female wrestler to claim the Raw Women’s Championship title and SmackDown Women’s Championship title- which made her a dual champion. Also, Lynch is the lengthiest Raw Women’s Champion. She held the title for 399 days before going on a pregnancy break on 11 May 2020. We have a wrestler who almost four years ago retired from professional wrestling at the suggestion of doctors due to various problems that caused him multiple concussions. Just a year after his return, Daniel Bryan was crowned WWE champion and starred in one of the best matches of WrestleMania 35. Daniel Bryan is undoubtedly one of the best athletes of the generation. He proved it this year by giving everything he has in the ring in every episode of SmackDown, in every PPV event, but especially even in the company’s non-televised events all over the world. Among Bryan’s most prominent matches, the fight against AJ Styles at Money in the Bank stands out. In modern wrestling, Lesnar is regarded as the most active fighter in the ring. Mostly scorned by fans, yet his results are undeniable and powerful in his royal rumble match this year, where he demonstrated his epic performance against several other fighters. He was in the ring for over an hour eliminating over thirteen different opponents as they came in. Lesnar may not have won the title, but he tied the record for most eliminations in a Royal Rumble match ever. Seth Rollins is the man WWE chose to be its biggest star after the rejection and temporary departure of Roman Reigns. Rollins is tough by nature, a typical hero in the ring, and he has fought every day to earn his place as one of the greats in the world of wrestling. Among his achievements last year is the fight against AJ Styles at Money in the Bank, as well as the last PPV of The Shield in FastLane, the classic fight of Survivor Series in the RAW team, and the fight in SummerSlam against Brock Lesnar. Make way for the queen of Wrestling as she claims her deserved place on the podium of the best female wrestlers of today. Charlotte Flair is without a doubt the best female fighter. Her physical prowess and ability allow her to handle a technical style with a touch of flying. She is the daughter of legendary wrestler Ric Flair. It is the charisma of the legendary Ric Flair but with impeccable skill in the ring. After her debut in 2013, she had stopped at nothing but winning every single important match. She won the NXT Women’s Champion title in 2015, Raw Women’s Champion 2016, SmackDown Women’s Champion 2018, and undefeated in WrestleMania. If someone tells you to think of promising young wrestlers, you will immediately think of Adam Cole. He is the longest-serving champion in NXT history and is considered by many to be the company’s icon, above Johnny Gargano. Cole’s career began in ROH and NXT (similar to Daniel Bryan’s), and now he is one of the most iconic stables of today. Being so young, he has a h

She has won the SmackDown Women’s Champion title a total of three times in her career. Becky Lynch made her appearance on the cover page of ESPN magazine-making her the first wrestler to be featured. Lynch is not just a professional fighter but a born champion as she is the only female wrestler to claim the Raw Women’s Championship title and SmackDown Women’s Championship title- which made her a dual champion. Also, Lynch is the lengthiest Raw Women’s Champion. She held the title for 399 days before going on a pregnancy break on 11 May 2020. We have a wrestler who almost four years ago retired from professional wrestling at the suggestion of doctors due to various problems that caused him multiple concussions. Just a year after his return, Daniel Bryan was crowned WWE champion and starred in one of the best matches of WrestleMania 35. Daniel Bryan is undoubtedly one of the best athletes of the generation. He proved it this year by giving everything he has in the ring in every episode of SmackDown, in every PPV event, but especially even in the company’s non-televised events all over the world. Among Bryan’s most prominent matches, the fight against AJ Styles at Money in the Bank stands out. In modern wrestling, Lesnar is regarded as the most active fighter in the ring. Mostly scorned by fans, yet his results are undeniable and powerful in his royal rumble match this year, where he demonstrated his epic performance against several other fighters. He was in the ring for over an hour eliminating over thirteen different opponents as they came in. Lesnar may not have won the title, but he tied the record for most eliminations in a Royal Rumble match ever. Seth Rollins is the man WWE chose to be its biggest star after the rejection and temporary departure of Roman Reigns. Rollins is tough by nature, a typical hero in the ring, and he has fought every day to earn his place as one of the greats in the world of wrestling. Among his achievements last year is the fight against AJ Styles at Money in the Bank, as well as the last PPV of The Shield in FastLane, the classic fight of Survivor Series in the RAW team, and the fight in SummerSlam against Brock Lesnar. Make way for the queen of Wrestling as she claims her deserved place on the podium of the best female wrestlers of today. Charlotte Flair is without a doubt the best female fighter. Her physical prowess and ability allow her to handle a technical style with a touch of flying. She is the daughter of legendary wrestler Ric Flair. It is the charisma of the legendary Ric Flair but with impeccable skill in the ring. After her debut in 2013, she had stopped at nothing but winning every single important match. She won the NXT Women’s Champion title in 2015, Raw Women’s Champion 2016, SmackDown Women’s Champion 2018, and undefeated in WrestleMania. If someone tells you to think of promising young wrestlers, you will immediately think of Adam Cole. He is the longest-serving champion in NXT history and is considered by many to be the company’s icon, above Johnny Gargano. Cole’s career began in ROH and NXT (similar to Daniel Bryan’s), and now he is one of the most iconic stables of today. Being so young, he has a historic level fight, especially the one against Gargano at TakeOver Toronto 2019. Along with being the longest reign in NXT history, he won four awards at the NXT Awards. Drew Mcintyre has been a consistent fighter over the last decade, and he is currently at the peak of his wrestling career. Drew has fought several championships in WWE, one of which was with Brock Lesnar for the WWE title in WWE Performance Center in Orlando, Florida, with no fans in attendance. He won against Brock Lesna to claim the title for the first time in his career. Drew Mcintyre has proven that he can do everything, including jumping out of the ring and landing on his back. Most times, it is surprising fans to see a man of 1.96 meters, and 120 kilos do that. Being phenomenal for this man is not a slogan; it is his reality. The Bullet Club’s second leader was the best in TNA (Impact), he was the best in NJPW, and he is the best WWE wrestler right now. We see him make a fast-paced aerial move with a strong powerhouse lift and finish off with an agile combination of punches. He was named Wrestler of the Decade by Pro Wrestling Illustrated, he was GrandSlam Champion in TNA and ROH, maximum champion in NJPW 2 times, and has won every single WWE main cast individual belt. In the ’70s, WWE had Ric Flair; in the 80’s they had Sting and Bret Hart; in the 90’s they had Shawn Michales and Undertaker. But all those who grew up watching Wrestling in the 21st century have AJ Styles as their best WWE superstar. There you have it, our selection of the best Wrestlers in the WWE as of 2020. It goes without saying that these fantastic WWE Superstars are definitely at the top of their game and have been for several years now. They will continue to mesmerize the crowd with their incredible performances. Who do you think should have made it into this list? Please let us know in the comments section. This article was created by a combination of a few writers and editors from Sporty Tell. It's not rare for our writers and team members to collaborate and work together to create the articles you read on the site. This is just one of these examples. This is why you don't see just one author for this article.

title: Best Technical Wrestlers In WWE History
meta_description: These WWE names stood out as technical wizards, showcasing their extraordinary ability in the ring.
headings: Natalya May Be The Greatest Female Technical Wrestler WWE Has Ever Seen
Gunther's Championship Reigns Have Been Defined By His Technical Prowess
The Dragon Was A Technical Great, Particularly Among Babyfaces
The Million Dollar Man Is A Solid In-Ring Technician
The Heartbreak Kid Is One Of The All-Time Greats
The Nature Boy Is A 16-time World Champion
The Macho Man Is An Incredible Grappler And High-Flyer
Jericho Reinvented His Character Several Times
Latino Heat Won The WWE Championship In 2004
content: There aren’t many wrestlers during this WWE era who can hold a candle to the technicians of yesteryear. But that is because the wrestling industry has moved farther and farther away from its roots both in amateur and the original professional wrestling model. The root of two guys telling a story through a Kayfabe fight is that they’re both trying to win through wrestling. Over the years, WWE has had many wrestlers who could deliver technical masterpieces in the ring. Here are the best technical matches in WWE history. That’s a bit of a shame since the very best mat technicians are responsible for some of the most memorable matches of all time. The very best technical wrestlers have always been able to meld the best aspects of sports entertainment storytelling with old-school mat wrestling. There are those professional wrestling fans who love to watch plunder-filled brawls or who are most captivated by high spots coming in from the top rope. While there's nothing wrong with those elements of sports entertainment, it remains the case throughout the ages that technical wrestling is the foundational component of the pro wrestling endeavor. Indeed, wrestlers who stand the test of time tend to be ones who know how to mix it up on the mat with reversals, holds, and expertly applied pinning predicaments. That's true right up to the modern day when a key part of the longevity of a star like Natalya, not to mention the repeated success of a performer like Gunther comes down to just how technically proficient they are. It's well-documented that Natalya's WWE career holds a unique place in history for its longevity and spanning an era when women's wrestling was such an afterthought all the way to the present day when the division is in the spotlight. One point that doesn't get highlighted enough is that The Queen of Harts has earned her place on the roster by virtue of sheer technical skill. Natalya is from the last generation of wrestlers with a legitimate claim to coming up through the Hart Dungeon. She carries forward that tradition nicely as a technically savvy performer who can go hold for hold with anyone, highlighted by her expert application of her family's own Sharpshooter finisher. Yes, Gunther's chops strike fear in the hearts of his opponents. The key underpinning of his long championship reigns in WWE, however, has largely connected back to his technical skills. Billed as The Ring General, the big man has an array of submission holds at his disposal. More to the point, his offense flows with a sense of realism and smoothness that so few wrestlers can compare with. From Walter to Gunther, WWE's reigning World Heavyweight Champion has undergone a shocking body transformation. Everything Gunther does in the ring is tinged with a sense of realism. Indeed, while he accents his offense with modern insertions like the power bomb, his nasty inflection on the Boston Crab and other wrestling classics underscore that he's an old-school technician at heart. When evaluating wrestlers based on ring work alone, and all more so when narrowing in on pure babyfaces, Ricky Steamboat has to rate among the very best who ever set foot in a WWE ring. Though he often used high-flying maneuvers for his most famous offense, he was also uncommonly smooth on the mat, including having what may have been the greatest arm drag in the history of pro wrestling. Indeed, anyone skeptical Steamboat's technical prowess need look no further than his all-time classic with Randy Savage at WrestleMania 3. While the encounter included some heated brawling, it's the two men going counter for counter, reversing pinning predicaments that rendered so much drama and that so many performers have aimed to imitate over the decades to follow with mixed results. "The Million Dollar Man" Ted DiBiase is one of the greatest heels in WWE history to never win the WWE Championship. DiBiase had chances to win the WWE Championship but he was unable to beat the likes of "Macho Man" Randy Savage and Hulk Hogan for the title. Nevertheless, DiBiase was one of the best heels in the '80s, and he had a solid run in WWE leading him to become a Hall of Famer in 2010. DiBiase had an old-school style of technical wrestling and he would wear down opponents with submission holds. The Million Dollar Dream was his finisher, and DiBiase wasn't afraid to get physical by using his fists in his matches. DiBiase doesn't get credit for his in-ring skills as he's more remembered for his gimmick and laugh. Shawn Michaels is unquestionably one of the greatest wrestlers of all time. Michaels' in-ring skills are second to none, and he has featured in some legendary wrestling matches that will live in infamy. The Heartbreak Kid is a four-time WWE World Champion, and he has shown that he is a brawler as he brawled with the likes of Diesel and Mankind in his WWE Championship matches in 1996. Michaels has brilliant agility, and he hasn't shied away from doing high-flying moves. Michaels is a go

DiBiase had an old-school style of technical wrestling and he would wear down opponents with submission holds. The Million Dollar Dream was his finisher, and DiBiase wasn't afraid to get physical by using his fists in his matches. DiBiase doesn't get credit for his in-ring skills as he's more remembered for his gimmick and laugh. Shawn Michaels is unquestionably one of the greatest wrestlers of all time. Michaels' in-ring skills are second to none, and he has featured in some legendary wrestling matches that will live in infamy. The Heartbreak Kid is a four-time WWE World Champion, and he has shown that he is a brawler as he brawled with the likes of Diesel and Mankind in his WWE Championship matches in 1996. Michaels has brilliant agility, and he hasn't shied away from doing high-flying moves. Michaels is a good grappler, and he has some submission holds in his repertoire by using the Sharpshooter and Figure Four Leglock in some instances. Ric Flair is a legend in the professional business and he's got a catalog of memorable wrestling matches. The Nature Boy is a 16-time World Champion and he's wrestled in many wrestling promotions and locked it up with the very best in the business. The two-time WWE Hall of Famer's Figure Four Leglock has been his key submission finisher which has seen him win multiple World Championships. Flair is the Dirtiest Player in the Game, and his matches with Randy Savage over the WWE Championship and his retirement match with Shawn Michaels at WrestleMania 24 were some of the greatest battles in his iconic career. Unquestionably, "Macho Man" Randy Savage is one of the greatest wrestlers ever. CM Punk and Bayley are fans of Savage and have emulated his work. Savage is known for being an incredible high-flyer with his trademark elbow drop off the top rope as his finisher. Savage was more than capable of holding his own in the ring. He's a superb grappler, and he carried The Ultimate Warrior in their WrestleMania 7 encounter. Savage had a great look and the former two-time WWE Champion beat Ted DiBiase and Ric Flair for both of his WWE Championship reigns at WrestleMania. Chris Jericho is an intelligent in-ring competitor, and Y2J has always been technically sound inside the squared circle. Jericho moved from WCW to WWE in August 1999, and Jericho settled into WWE right away. Jericho is a record-setting nine-time Intercontinental Champion, and he became the first-ever Undisputed WWE Champion at Vengeance 2001 when he defeated The Rock and Steve Austin back-to-back. The World Heavyweight Championship saw many amazing titleholders, but who were the best WWE wrestlers to hold the coveted Big Gold Belt? Jericho can mat wrestle and learned some invaluable knowledge when he was under Stu Hart's Learning Tree in the Dungeon. Jericho's a submission specialist with his trademark Walls of Jericho and Liontamer as some of the submission moves he has used to win countless championships. Eddie Guerrero's untimely death in 2005 sent shockwaves throughout the world, and there was an outpour of emotion when Eddie's colleagues, family, friends, and fans heard the news. Guerrero's short WWE career only lasted five years, but he left a catalog of memorable matches. Guerrero's technique inside the ring was enthralling to watch. Eddie was a member of the legendary Guerrero family and in his arsenal, he had some high-flying moves and submission holds. Guerrero mixed it up with the very best, and winning the WWE Championship in 2004 was a highlight of his career. Out of everyone on today’s WWE roster, there is no more accomplished ring technician than The Master Of The Alpha Academy - Chad Gable. First alongside Jason Jordan in American Alpha and the past year or so with Otis as the Alpha Academy, and a Shorty G or two in between, Gable puts on solid in-ring technical marvels every night. Like fellow Olympian before him, Kurt Angle, Gable has also “mastered” putting together the goofy parts of Sports Entertainment with the more serious aspects of in-ring action as well. Gable's the leader of American Made with The Creed Brothers and Ivy Nile as his stablemates. There's no margin for error with Gable expecting perfection, and he isn't afraid to slap his stablemates into shape.

title: The 50 Best Wrestlers in WWE Right Now, Ranked by Wrestling Fans
meta_description: Over 17K sports fans have voted on the 50+ athletes on Best Current Wrestlers in the WWE. Current Top 3: Cody Rhodes, Roman Reigns, Randy Orton
content: Who are the most famous WWE wrestlers? While the days of "Stone Cold" Steve Austin and "Macho Man" Randy Savage are gone, there are still plenty of current WWE wrestlers worth watching—to say the least. Sure, nothing can beat those golden years when it was still WWF and Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson was cookin' and doin' his thing, but the world of wrestling continues to bring it. From the top highflyers to the ultimate big men, the top WWE wrestlers today bring new moves, revisit the classics, and put on a great show nonetheless—lest we forget the always-anticipated finisher moves. Comprising much of the Raw and Smackdown bills, including tag teams within each promotion, the most popular WWE wrestlers right now are some of the toughest, scariest, and ruthless superstars of the ring. And while no one's forgot NXT and the Cruiserweight class, this WWE wrestlers list only features the best wrestlers who've proven themselves beyond development brands and special classes. So, who do you think is the best wrestler in the WWE right now? Check out the list of current WWE wrestlers and cast your votes for who you think top the rest. What Makes Cody Rhodes The Best Today: A versatile and passionate talent, Cody Rhodes has risen through the ranks of professional wrestling to become one of WWE's most respected figures. Not only does he possess an impressive in-ring skill set, but his natural charisma and captivating storytelling abilities have secured him a prominent position within the company. As the son of legendary wrestler Dusty Rhodes, he carries on his family's legacy while forging his own path towards greatness. Cody Rhodes’s Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the AEW TNT Championship twice - Winning the Intercontinental Championship in WWE - Co-founding All Elite Wrestling (AEW) - Winning the ROH World Six-Man Tag Team Championship What Makes Randy Orton The Best Wrestler Today: Randy Orton, son of wrestling legend Cowboy Bob Orton, has been mesmerizing WWE audiences with his cold, calculating demeanor and smooth wrestling style since his debut in 2002. Known as The Viper, Orton strikes fear in the hearts of his opponents with his methodical prowess and lethal RKO finisher, seemingly coming out of nowhere to deliver the ultimate blow. Throughout his illustrious career, Orton has amassed an incredible 14 world championships, Royal Rumble victory, and Money in the Bank win, cementing his status among wrestling's all-time greats. His storied feuds with John Cena, Edge, and Triple H have left an indelible mark on WWE history. Randy Orton's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Youngest person to win the World Heavyweight Championship, defeating Chris Benoit at SummerSlam 2004 - Winning the Royal Rumble matches in 2009 and 2017 - Becoming a 14-time World Champion - Winning the first-ever WWE Championship Elimination Chamber match What Makes Roman Reigns The Best Wrestler Today: Roman Reigns has become one of the most polarizing figures in WWE today, despite being at the top of the company for several years now. Earning his place amongst wrestling royalty, Reigns' hard-hitting Samoan style comprises a mixture of agility, power, and incredible athletic prowess, often leaving opponents feeling the wrath of his devastating Spear finisher. With an impressive array of accolades under his belt, including four-time WWE World Heavyweight Champion and main eventing WrestleMania on multiple occasions, Reigns has certainly etched his name in wrestling history. His bone-chilling feuds with Brock Lesnar, Seth Rollins, and Triple H undoubtedly serve as a testament to the Tribal Chief's commitment to entertaining WWE fans around the globe. Roman Reigns's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the 2015 Royal Rumble - Defeating Triple H at Wrestlemania 32 to win the WWE World Heavyweight Championship - Battling Leukemia twice and returning to the ring successfully each time - Becoming a four-time WWE World Heavyweight Champion What Makes Rhea Ripley The Best Wrestler Today: Rhea Ripley has burst onto the scene in WWE with her powerful presence, brutal wrestling style, and undeniable charisma. Known for her aggressive, hard-hitting approach, Ripley dismantles opponents with her devastating Riptide finisher. As a former NXT and NXT UK Women's Champion and current Women's World Champion, Rhea's star is unmistakably on the rise. Her memorable battles with Shayna Baszler, Charlotte Flair, and Io Shk have showcased her incredible talent and potential, making her one to watch in WWE's women's division. Rhea Ripley's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the NXT UK Women's Championship - Winning the NXT Women's Championship at NXT: TakeOver - WarGames in 2019 - Becoming the RAW Women’s Champion by defeating Asuka at Wrestlemania 37 - Winning the inaugural NXT UK Women's Championship What Makes Seth Rollins The Best Wrestler Today: Seth Rollins is widely regarded as one of the industry's most significant ta

And while no one's forgot NXT and the Cruiserweight class, this WWE wrestlers list only features the best wrestlers who've proven themselves beyond development brands and special classes. So, who do you think is the best wrestler in the WWE right now? Check out the list of current WWE wrestlers and cast your votes for who you think top the rest. What Makes Cody Rhodes The Best Today: A versatile and passionate talent, Cody Rhodes has risen through the ranks of professional wrestling to become one of WWE's most respected figures. Not only does he possess an impressive in-ring skill set, but his natural charisma and captivating storytelling abilities have secured him a prominent position within the company. As the son of legendary wrestler Dusty Rhodes, he carries on his family's legacy while forging his own path towards greatness. Cody Rhodes’s Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the AEW TNT Championship twice - Winning the Intercontinental Championship in WWE - Co-founding All Elite Wrestling (AEW) - Winning the ROH World Six-Man Tag Team Championship What Makes Randy Orton The Best Wrestler Today: Randy Orton, son of wrestling legend Cowboy Bob Orton, has been mesmerizing WWE audiences with his cold, calculating demeanor and smooth wrestling style since his debut in 2002. Known as The Viper, Orton strikes fear in the hearts of his opponents with his methodical prowess and lethal RKO finisher, seemingly coming out of nowhere to deliver the ultimate blow. Throughout his illustrious career, Orton has amassed an incredible 14 world championships, Royal Rumble victory, and Money in the Bank win, cementing his status among wrestling's all-time greats. His storied feuds with John Cena, Edge, and Triple H have left an indelible mark on WWE history. Randy Orton's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Youngest person to win the World Heavyweight Championship, defeating Chris Benoit at SummerSlam 2004 - Winning the Royal Rumble matches in 2009 and 2017 - Becoming a 14-time World Champion - Winning the first-ever WWE Championship Elimination Chamber match What Makes Roman Reigns The Best Wrestler Today: Roman Reigns has become one of the most polarizing figures in WWE today, despite being at the top of the company for several years now. Earning his place amongst wrestling royalty, Reigns' hard-hitting Samoan style comprises a mixture of agility, power, and incredible athletic prowess, often leaving opponents feeling the wrath of his devastating Spear finisher. With an impressive array of accolades under his belt, including four-time WWE World Heavyweight Champion and main eventing WrestleMania on multiple occasions, Reigns has certainly etched his name in wrestling history. His bone-chilling feuds with Brock Lesnar, Seth Rollins, and Triple H undoubtedly serve as a testament to the Tribal Chief's commitment to entertaining WWE fans around the globe. Roman Reigns's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the 2015 Royal Rumble - Defeating Triple H at Wrestlemania 32 to win the WWE World Heavyweight Championship - Battling Leukemia twice and returning to the ring successfully each time - Becoming a four-time WWE World Heavyweight Champion What Makes Rhea Ripley The Best Wrestler Today: Rhea Ripley has burst onto the scene in WWE with her powerful presence, brutal wrestling style, and undeniable charisma. Known for her aggressive, hard-hitting approach, Ripley dismantles opponents with her devastating Riptide finisher. As a former NXT and NXT UK Women's Champion and current Women's World Champion, Rhea's star is unmistakably on the rise. Her memorable battles with Shayna Baszler, Charlotte Flair, and Io Shk have showcased her incredible talent and potential, making her one to watch in WWE's women's division. Rhea Ripley's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the NXT UK Women's Championship - Winning the NXT Women's Championship at NXT: TakeOver - WarGames in 2019 - Becoming the RAW Women’s Champion by defeating Asuka at Wrestlemania 37 - Winning the inaugural NXT UK Women's Championship What Makes Seth Rollins The Best Wrestler Today: Seth Rollins is widely regarded as one of the industry's most significant talents, showcasing unmatched athleticism, technical skill, and charisma. Rollins has walked a path to glory in WWE, adopting an innovative high-flying style that often leaves fans in awe as he executes his trademark Curb Stomp, ripping through opponents like a tornado. The Architect has amassed an impressive list of accomplishments, such as two-time WWE Universal Champion, two-time WWE Champion, and winning the 2019 Royal Rumble, proving to be the epitome of success in the squared circle. His intense rivalries with wrestlers like Dean Ambrose and Bray Wyatt have generated some of the most memorable moments in modern WWE history. Seth Rollins's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the 2019 Royal Rumble - Capturing the WWE Championship and the United States Championship at the same time in 2015 - Winning the NXT Championship in 2012 - Cashing in his Money in the Bank contract during WrestleMania 31 main event and winning the WWE World Heavyweight Championship What Makes CM Punk The Best Today: Known for his outspoken views and unapologetic attitude, CM Punk has consistently pushed boundaries both inside and outside the ring. His undeniable talent as a wrestler is only matched by his ability to captivate audiences with intense promos that showcase his intelligence and wit. With an impressive resume of championships under his belt, CM Punk continues to prove that he is a force to be reckoned with in the world of professional wrestling. CM Punk's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the WWE Championship twice - Winning the WWE World Heavyweight Championship three times - Winning the Money in the Bank ladder match in 2008 and 2009 - Having one of the longest WWE Championship reigns in modern history

title: 10 Best Football Players To Ever Wrestle In WWE, Ranked
meta_description: These athletes didn't have much success in football, but did pretty well when they moved on o wrestling.
content: Many a wrestler has a previous background in football. Due to the size of the athletes and athleticism from football, making the transition to the world of wrestling a natural move. The likes of Brock Lesnar and Roman Reigns have made this transition seamlessly and are currently two of the top stars in WWE today. Whilst there have been a plethora of legends who also made the transition from the football field. Many have even played in the NFL and had a solid career before joining the WWE. So let's explore the best football players to ever wrestle inside a WWE ring. One of the most electrifying entertainers in wrestling history once had a career in football. Rock was viewed as a promising prospect as he signed with the University of Miami, however, he suffered numerous injuries. Related: 10 Football Players That Would Shine In Pro Wrestling This halted his football career as he signed on with the Calgary Stampeders for the Canadian Football League. He only lasted two months into the season before he was released and moved into pro wrestling. Baron Corbin plays a slimy heel currently in WWE. He is clearly highly thought of within the company due to his consistent TV time over the last few years. Before this, Corbin was a part of NXT after being signed up after his release from football. He was a part of Indianapolis Colts and Arizona Cardinals during a two-year spell from 2009 to 2011. This was after he impressed in the Divison II National Championships during his college days. This list is based on football ability rather than wrestling ability, otherwise, Rawley would not make this list. Rawley is widely known to be friends with Rob Gronkowski after playing with his brother during his college days. He was a part of the Green Bay Packers after impressing during a tryout in which he and two others were brought in from a group of 25. He also played for the Arizona Cardinals before an injury put him on the sidelines. Pillman was a fan favorite during his time in WWE, receiving huge pops any time he appeared in a wrestling ring. Sadly, Pillman could have been a megastar but passed away in 1997 aged 35. Before his wrestling career, he was apart of his hometown club Cincinnati Bengals. He impressed so much that he won the Ed Block Courage Award and was handed a contract for Buffalo Bills. Lesnar went down a different path than most on this list. Instead of moving from football to wrestling, he went the opposite way. Quitting WWE to try and realise his dream of playing in the NFL. He joined the Minnesota Viking after impressing during the NFL Combine. Lesnar appeared in many pre-season games and was offered the chance to represent the Vikings in NFL Europa but he turned it down and moved into MMA. During his college days, Reigns was voted Defensive Player Of The Year by Pensacola News Journal. He was eventually signed by Minnesota Vikings however he was shortly released after being diagnosed with leukaemia. Reigns recovered and joined Jacksonville Jaguars but again was released early but he did forge a career in the Canadian Football League. However, Reigns retired early and followed his family lineage into wrestling. Goldberg is one of the most intimidating men in wrestling history. His undefeated streak in WCW was iconic and he redeemed himself in WWE during his return feud with Brock Lesnar. He was drafted by NFL side LA Rams after impressing during his time in Georgia. Before moving on to Atlanta Falcons where Goldberg played 14 times in the NFL. However, his football career ended prematurely due to injury. A Hall Of Famer and multi-time champion within WWE, Ron Simmons wrestling career is a memorable one. However, his football career was also impressive as he was elected into the College Football Hall Of Fame after thriving for Florida State University. This resulted in him being snapped up by NFL side Cleveland Browns who he was with from 1981-82. Until he moved into the USFL with the Tampa Bay Bandits where he was teammates with Lex Luger. Vader is widely regarded as one of the most agile big men in wrestling history. He wrestled all over the US and Japan before finally coming to the WWE. Before his legendary wrestling career, he was a successful football player. Related: 10 Unbelievable Photos Of Current WWE Wrestlers Before They Were Stars He was signed by NFL side LA Rams and during his first season, he helped guide them to the Super Bowl earning an NFC Championship Ring in the process. However, he was forced to retire early due to injury Monty Brown is by far and away the best footballer to compete in a WWE ring. During his early football career, he was named MVP and Co-Player Of The Year for Ferris State Bulldogs. This resulted in him being signed by Buffalo Bills where he played professionally and competed in the Super Bowl. He then joined the New England Patriots, however, he only joined to be closer to the WWE Headquarters. Next: 10 WWE Superstars You Didn't Know Used To Play Football

title: Wrestling Inc. | Pro Wrestling News, WWE & AEW Results, Superstar Interviews
meta_description: The latest WWE, AEW, NJPW real-time results, news, exclusive interviews, live coverage, and more - since 1997.
headings: UFC Fight Night 270: Evloev Vs. Murphy Full Fight Card & Preview
WWE SmackDown 3/20/2026: 3 Things We Hated And 3 Things We Loved
WWE SmackDown Results 3/20 - We Hear From Randy Orton, Three Championships On The Line & More
Injured AEW Star Worked Backstage Role During Dynamite & Collision Taping
WWE Reportedly Slaps Tama Tonga With 'Significant' Fine Due To Rehearsal Video Post
Backstage Report On WWE Discounting Tickets, Including WrestleMania 42
WWE's Bayley Shares Her Initial Reaction To Hugger Theme Music: 'I Hated It'
Chelsea Green Reflects On Her Two WWE Women's US Championship Runs
Former NJPW Star EVIL Reportedly Wants To Begin WWE Career In NXT
Dennis Condrey Of The Midnight Express Dies At 74, AEW's Dax Harwood Opens GoFundMe
AEW's Swerve Strickland Says 'Evolution' Is A Key Part Of His Wrestling Persona
WWE HOFer Jim Ross Doesn't Believe Stone Cold Steve Austin Will Wrestle Again
AEW's Billy Gunn Reveals He Got In Trouble Over Funniest Moment Of His WWE Career
Former Producer David Sahadi Details His WWE Exit & Tense Dynamic With Kevin Dunn
WWE Hall Of Famer JBL Reflects On Split With Former APA Tag Partner Ron Simmons
Stone Cold Steve Austin Explains Why He Was 'Always Mad' When Wrestling Vince McMahon
Thekla Explains Mutual Benefits Of AEW Partnership With Julia Hart & Skye Blue
Tony Schiavone Says Former ROH World Champion Is Underused In AEW
content: UFC Fight Night hits London, England with a card headlined by Movsar Evloev versus Lerone Murphy at the O2 Arena. On the night of a wild Drew McIntyre vs. Jacob Fatu brawl, here are three things we hated & three things we loved about the 3/20/26 episode of "WWE SmackDown!" This is Wrestling Inc.’s results for “WWE SmackDown” on March 20, 2026, with the WWE Tag Team & Women's Tag Team Championships up for grabs! Another AEW star is nearing a return from injury as they were backstage for "AEW Dynamite" and "AEW Collision." Former two-time WWE Tag Team Champion Tama Tonga is in hot water after posting a rehearsal video from last week's "WWE SmackDown." WWE held a discount sale on March 16 for a number of shows including WrestleMania 42, and looking at the figures, it looks to have worked well. Bayley reveals that she despised her theme song as the Hugger, but chose to keep it after discovering that management loved it. WWE star Chelsea Green opens up about her approaches to her two reigns as Women's United States Champion. EVIL looks all but set to join WWE in the near future, but the former NJPW star wants to start in "WWE NXT" rather than the main roster. Former founding member of the Midnight Express, Dennis Condrey, passed away Friday evening at 74. AEW's Dax Harwood creates GoFundMe page for his late idol. "The Most Dangerous Man in AEW" Swerve Strickland said that evolution is a key part to his wrestling persona, especially over the last four years in AEW. WWE Hall of Famer and AEW commentator Jim Ross doesn't believe that fellow Hall of Famer Stone Cold Steve Austin will ever wrestle a match again Billy Gunn reflects on upsetting Vince McMahon in what he considers the most hilarious moment of his career in WWE. David Sahadi has discussed his WWE exit in the early 2000s and how he believes Kevin Dunn negatively influenced his relationship with Vince McMahon. WWE HOFer JBL doesn't think he'd be where he is, if not for Ron SImmons. WWE Hall of Famer "Stone Cold" Steve Austin used to get fired up over one thing about Vince McMahon whenever they shared the ring. AEW Women's World Champion Thekla explains the mutual benefits of her partnership with Triangle of Madness stablemates Julia Hart and Skye Blue AEW broadcaster Tony Schiavone recently highlighted one performer the company has under contract that he feels is not featured enough. The March 18 episode of "AEW Dynamite" saw the fallout from AEW Revolution, and there was a lot of buzz in the TV ratings. The Bella Twins saw their first shot at the Women's Tag Team Championships end in disqualification during "WWE SmackDown." Carmelo Hayes defeated Ilja Dragunov for the Men's US title last December. Michin and B-Fab revealed on "WWE SmackDown" that they now have Jade Cargill's back, and beat down her WrestleMania 42 opponent, Rhea Ripley, in the ring. UFC announced it will be working with the Association of Boxing Commissions for the White House card, though the DC Combat Sports Commission are not impressed. President Donald Trump has been promoting the UFC's upcoming White House event, Freedom 250, with many within his Republican circle clamoring for tickets. Cody Rhodes is not angry with rapper Travis Scott for the slap he landed on him in 2025, claiming that it wasn’t the hardest slap he has received in WWE. Damian Priest and R-Truth dethroned the MFTs to win the WWE Tag Team Championship on "WWE SmackDown." Tyson Fury has joined the growing list of heavyweights interested in a bout with Jon Jones. According to a report, at least one advertised match on "WWE SmackDown" is no longer scheduled to take place. The Bella Twins discussed their match for the WWE Women's Tag Team Championships tonight on "WWE SmackDown" in length on the "Nikki & Brie Show." Paul "Triple H" Levesque announced that Nickelback's latest song, their first since 2022, will be an official theme of WrestleMania 42.

title: List of WWE personnel - Wikipedia
content: WWE is an American entertainment company and professional wrestling promotion based in Stamford, Connecticut.[1] It is owned and operated by TKO Group Holdings, itself a majority owned subsidiary of Endeavor Group Holdings. WWE personnel consists of professional wrestlers, managers, play-by-play and color commentators, ring announcers, interviewers, referees, trainers, producers, script writers, and various other positions. Executives and board members are also listed. As of 2024, WWE employs over 800 full-time employees as part of its business operations, spread across its three segments: Media, Live Events, and Consumer Products. In addition, they contract with various other vendors and service providers.[2] As part of its Live Events segment, WWE signs performers known as professional wrestlers; these performers are described by the company as "independent contractors",[1] with contracts for performers typically ranging from short developmental to multi-year deals.[3][4] Since the reintroduction of the WWE brand extension in 2016, performers are assigned to one of two primary "main roster" brands: Raw and SmackDown. A third brand, NXT, had originally been designated as a developmental brand for the main roster. In 2019, it was elevated to the third main roster brand.[5][6][7] In 2021, the brand's weekly television show was rebooted as "NXT 2.0", and was reinstated to its original status as the developmental division for the main roster (Raw and SmackDown), although it has since dropped the "2.0" name.[8] In 2025, WWE introduced a fourth brand, called Evolve, which features wrestlers who are just beginning their training at the WWE Performance Center under the WWE NIL program, as well as wrestlers in independent promotions that are part of the WWE ID program, with the goal of advancing to NXT.[9] Main roster performers primarily appear on Monday Night Raw and Friday Night SmackDown. Advanced developmental wrestlers appear on NXT, while beginning developmental wrestlers appear on Evolve. Supplementary shows include Main Event, which features Raw and SmackDown wrestlers (with occasional appearances from NXT wrestlers), and LFG, which features Evolve wrestlers. Performers may also appear on other WWE weekly television programming, as well as on its Premium Live Events and untelevised house shows. A majority of personnel in its developmental system train at the WWE Performance Center in Orlando, Florida. For the most part, the brands have their own distinct championships, although some select titles are open to all the brands, such as the WWE Speed Championship for male wrestlers, as well as the WWE Women's Speed Championship and WWE Women's Tag Team Championship for female wrestlers. WWE wrestlers holding championships from Mexican sister promotion Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA), U.S. partner promotion Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA), and Japanese partner promotion Pro Wrestling Noah (Noah) are also recognized by WWE. WWE refers to talent not specifically assigned to a brand as "free agents", who may appear on any show.[174] There are several announcers who cover WWE broadcasts. This group includes backstage interviewers, presenters, ringside commentators, ring announcers, pre-show hosts, and analysts. Select former or retired WWE wrestlers not on the main roster (sometimes billed as "WWE Legends") and other celebrities and personalities have contracts to make periodic appearances—either on WWE TV or for promotional events—serving as "ambassadors" (public representatives/spokespeople) for the company. As both NXT and Evolve are based at the Performance Center (PC), PC coaches and trainers also work as producers for those brands.

WWE Smackdown Live, March 20th, 2026, Watch-Wrestling FHD stream The Road to WrestleMania 42 intensifies as Friday Night SmackDown heads to the Lenovo Center in Raleigh, North Carolina, just one month before the “Showcase of the Immortals.” All eyes are on 14-time World Champion Randy Orton, who will finally break his silence following his brutal betrayal of WWE Champion Cody Rhodes. During last week’s WrestleMania contract signing, Orton savagely attacked his longtime friend and former protégé, leaving the American Nightmare battered. On Monday Night Raw, Orton declared himself a “killer,” hinting at a reminder of his ruthless roots and taking shots at wrestling’s royal families while addressing Michael Cole. Fans eagerly await his explanation for turning on the man he once called a brother. The night promises high-stakes action beyond Orton’s appearance. Fresh off winning a grueling Tag Team Turmoil Match to earn No. 1 contender status, Damian Priest and R-Truth challenge the dominant WWE Tag Team Champions, The MFTs, in a blockbuster title bout. Adding to the excitement, WWE Hall of Famers The Bella Twins—Nikki and Brie—make their return, gunning for the WWE Women’s Tag Team Championship. They’ll face powerhouse duo Nia Jax and Lash Legend, aiming to reclaim glory in the division. Watch SmackDown this Friday at 8 p.m. ET/7 CT on SmackDown on USA.
